content.full_text:
CalifOrnia
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 27, l99]_
Mr. Peter M. Detwi ler
senate Committee on Local Government
state Capitol, Roon 2085
P. O. Box 94248
sacramento, cA 94248-0001
Re: Your Request for Adv■
ce
Our Fi■e No. A-9■-040
Dear Mr. Detwiler:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
Political Reform Act (the trActr) . Your letter of January 77, L99l
posed two questions. In a subseguent letter dated January 30,
1991, you withdrew your second guestion and re-phrased your first
question. It is the January 30th letter to which thj-s letter
responds.
OUESTION
Are actual travel expenses provided to you by the California
Continuing Education of the Bar for attendance at quarterly
rneetings of the editors of the Cal-ifornia Land Use Ouarterlv
permissible under Section 8 95 06?
CONCLUSION
Reinbursement for actual travel expenses provided to you by
the California Continuing Education of the Bar for editorial
meetings for a quarterly 1aw journal are within the exclusion i,n
Section 89506, and are therefore permissible under Section
89506(a) (2), since your reimbursement will come from a bona fide
educational institution.
FACTS
You are a Cbnsultant to the senate Committee on Loca■
Governmento  You have been invited tO serve as one of a sma■
■
group Of executive editors of a proposed pub■
ication entitled
428 J Street,Suitc 800 ● P.0.Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804‐ 0807 ● (916)322・5660


california Land Use ouarterlvt A Journal of Land Use Law and
Social Policv (the "quarterly'r). 
ft is anticipated that the
topics covered by the quarterly will incl,ude housingT, Iand use
planning, environmental quality and other issues of concern to the
tegislatj-ve cornmittee on which you are a consultant.
Additional-ly, there wiLl be evaluation by the Editorial- Board of
pending and chaptered legislation.
There wilt be no f j,nancial compensation for your attendance
or participation in the editors' rneetings, but you har're been
offered reirnbursernent for your actual travel expenses. The
Unj-vers j.ty of California will be providing the reinbursement,
since the California Continuing Education of the Bar is under the
auspices of the university. 
You are concerned that j.n accepting
such reimbursement you nay be in violation of the Act.
ANALYSIS
Newly enacted Section 89506 provides that certain payments,
advances, and reimbursements for travel made to state elected
officers, members c,f state boards and commissions and designated
employees of state agencies are neither prohibited nor lirnited by
the Act. As a consul,tant to a legislative comrnittee, you are a
designated employee of a state agency.
Section 89506 sets forth the following in pertinent part:
(a) Pa)rnents, advances, or reimbursements,
for travel, including actual transportation and
related lodging and subsistence which is reasonably
related to a legislative or governmental purpose,
or to an issue of state, national, or international
public policy, is not prohibited or linited by thj.s
chapter if either of the following apply:
(2') The travel is provided by a government, a
governmental agency, a foreign governnent, a
governmentat authority, a bona fide public or
private educational institution, 
as defined in
section 2o3 of the Revenue and Taxation code, or a
nonprofit charitable or religious organization
which is exempt from taxation under Section
5oL(c) (3) of the Internal Revenue code, or by a
person dorniciled outside the United States srhich
substantially satisfies the reguirements for tax
exempt status under section 501(c) (3) of the
Internal Re-venue Code.


The threshotd question that must be answered is whether the
editorj.al meetings are reasonably related to a legislative or
governrnental purpose or to an issue of state, national or
international public policy. 
The topics which the quarterly will
examine are reLated to the legislative conmittee which you serve
as a consultant and the anticipated evaluation of pending and
chaptered legislation will very like1y be useful to you in your
legislative comnittee duties. Fina11y, the topics on v/hich the
quarterly expects to focus are issues of state public policy
relative to land use planningr. Thus, the work that you will be
doing for the quarterly appears to be related to a legislative or
governmental purpose.
The next questj.on is vhether reimbursement for travel
expenses by the continuing Education of the Bar meets the criteria
set forth in Section 89506(a) (2). A telephone conversation with
Johanna sherlin, internal editor of the quarterly for the
continuing Education of the Bar, has provided rne with the
inforrnation that your reimbursement will come from the University
of cal-ifornia. 
Since Section 89506(a) (2) permits travel
reimbursement provided by "a bona fide public or private
educationat institutiontr, as defined in Section 203 of the Revenue
and Taxation code, and since the University of California faLLs
within that definition, there should be no problem in connection
$iith your acceptance of such reimbursement.
f trust the above ans$rers your question. If you have need of
further information, please do not hesitate to call- ne at (9f6)
322-5901.
S incere ly,
Scott HalLabr
Acting cenera
counsel,
XP♪ム
Counsel, Lega
D ivi s ion
SLB:ken

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are actual travel expenses provided to you by the California
Continuing Education of the Bar for attendance at quarterly
rneetings of the editors of the Cal-ifornia Land Use Ouarterlv
permissible under Section 8 95 06?

CONCLUSION: Reinbursement for actual travel expenses provided to you by
the California Continuing Education of the Bar for editorial
meetings for a quarterly 1aw journal are within the exclusion i,n
Section 89506, and are therefore permissible under Section
89506(a) (2), since your reimbursement will come from a bona fide
educational institution.
